project.title: Application of harmonic FT-ICR-MS signal acquisition for improved proteome research
project.projectDescription: Proteomics experiments benefit from having both high resolving power and high scan speed. Harmonic detection provides a method to obtain twice the resolution for the same data acquisition period, or the same resolution in half the data acquisition period. We show that in a complex hela cell lysate sample, that we obtain 36% to 45% more unique peptides detected using harmonic detection with enhanced scan rate or resolution respectively.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Hela cells were grown to confluency in DMEM, and then harvested from the plate using PBS supplmented with EDTA. The pellet was then resuspended in a lysis bugger containing 100 mM ammonium bicarbonate and 8M Urea, and lysed by sonication. The resulting lysate was reduced with TCEP and alkylated with iodoacetamide. It was then digested overnight with trypsin at a 1:200 (w/w) ratio at 37C. The digest was then desalted using a sep-pak cartridge (Waters). The peptides were resuspended in 2% Acetonitrile/0.1% Formic acid for LC-MS/MS analysis.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Raw files were converted to mzXMLs using ReAdW. The mzXMLs were then searched with comet to generate .txt output files containin the top 5 hits per spectrum.
